fragility_points: Strictly higher than 70000 is required; equality is insufficient.
Binance-specific pricing or operational quirks matter more than broad BTC market consensus.
key_assumptions: Current ~74k Binance BTCUSDT level provides a sufficient buffer against the 70k strike.
No major macro or crypto-specific shock causes a 5%+ drawdown before the exact settlement minute.
Binance does not show a material exchange-specific downward dislocation at resolution.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket implies about 93.9% Yes for BTC above 70000 on Apr. 17.
Direct Binance data places BTC roughly 5.5% above the strike at research time.
Independent contextual price checks support that the cushion is real, not a Binance anomaly.
But the contract is exact-minute and exchange-specific, so path and operational risk are underpriced.
Therefore Yes remains favored, but fair confidence is lower than market confidence.
main_thesis: BTC is likely to stay above 70000 by Apr. 17 noon ET on Binance, but the market is somewhat overconfident because one-minute exact-time and one-exchange settlement create meaningful path risk.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon)... has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title.
source_quality_view: High-quality direct contract and Binance sources, with medium independence because settlement depends on one venue; cross-exchange verification improved confidence but did not remove path risk.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on a single Binance BTCUSDT 12:00 ET one-minute close, so a brief selloff or venue-specific dip can flip the result.
BTC can move 5%+ over two days, so the current cushion is meaningful but not invulnerable.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance BTCUSDT data around research time showed price near 74066 with recent 1m closes above 74000.
CoinGecko and Coinbase cross-checks were aligned near 74.1k.
timing_relevance: High: the market resolves on the Binance BTCUSDT one-minute close at 12:00 ET on Apr. 17, about 42 hours after research time.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much short-horizon volatility will compress or expand before Apr. 17 noon ET.
Whether any exchange-specific dislocation emerges on Binance near settlement.
what_would_change_view: I would move toward the market if BTC holds comfortably above ~73k into late Apr. 16 / early Apr. 17 with low volatility; I would move away if Binance BTCUSDT weakens toward 72k, diverges from other venues, or a shock raises the odds of a noon ET dip below 70k.
